observations: 2026-03-05: Submitted TopN solution. Logic is correct. Two commits: 'commit solution' + 'fixed tests' — suggests tests didn't pass first time, possibly n==0 edge case.
Slice aliasing bug: returns original slice when n >= len(nums) — not a copy.
Algorithm approach works but is O(n*k*logk); should know about min-heap (O(n*logk)) for future.
Test structure is table-driven (good habit), but assertions only check length in TestTopN — not actual values.
Commented-out dead test committed to repo — minor hygiene issue.
Missing test cases: n==0 with non-empty input, n==len(nums), negatives, duplicates.

2026-03-06: Submitted BankAccount (Exercise 2). Logic correct, pointer receiver reasoning articulated well. Key issues: implemented Print() instead of String() — missed fmt.Stringer interface; snake_case field names (account_number); no guard for negative withdrawal amount; error messages uppercase with 'Error:' prefix; shared mutable state across subtests.

2026-03-07: Submitted interfaces.go (Ex3) and errors.go (Ex4). Ex3: Shape interface correct, all shapes implemented correctly, Heron's formula right. Critical bug: LargestAreaShape returns minimum not maximum (ascending sort, returns index 0). Also mutates input slice — same aliasing pattern as Ex1. Function name mismatch (LargestAreaShape vs LargestArea). No tests. Ex4: Does not compile — ConfigError.Error() returns error not string. Major issues: loop exits after first key (returns inside loop), wrong key casing (Host/Port/Debug vs host/port/debug), no port range validation, no post-parse host-missing check, zero-value ConfigError{} used instead of nil for success, fmt.Errorf without %w wrapping.

2026-03-08: Submitted Ex3+Ex4 revision (commit 67ddcf6 'fixed some feedback'). Progress: Error() return type fixed, function signature fixed, added interfaces_test.go with good patterns (static interface checks, table-driven tests, edge cases). Remaining issues: errors.go STILL does not compile (return cfg,nil inside loop, no return after loop); wrong key casing still present (Host/Port/Debug vs host/port/debug); string(p_int) wrong conversion; missing port range validation; missing post-parse host check; no %w wrapping. interfaces.go: sort direction now correct; spurious copy(s,s_copy) added (pointless, overwrites original with identical data, shows Clone misunderstanding). Test: negative rectangle test will fail (-1*2=-2<0); typo TestShapeCaluclations; float equality without epsilon.
weak_spots: Slice aliasing / ownership semantics — recurring pattern (Ex1, Ex3, and now spurious copy in Ex3 fix)
Interface satisfaction (fmt.Stringer missed in Ex2; error interface return type fixed in Ex4 v2)
Go naming conventions (camelCase for fields, function name spec compliance)
Loop control flow — early return inside iteration loop (Ex4, persists in v2)
Nil vs zero-value for error returns
Type conversions — string(int64) is rune conversion, not decimal string
strong_spots: Pointer vs value receiver reasoning — articulated correctly
Correct algorithm choices: bufio.Scanner, strings.Cut, strconv.ParseBool, Heron's formula
Self-documentation via commented alternative implementations (shows thinking)
Adopted static interface compliance checks (var _ Shape = ...) — excellent senior pattern
Adding tests with revision — shows growth in workflow discipline

history.verdict: pass ex2 with notes — continue to ex3/4/5
ex3 logic mostly right but critical sort bug + mutation; ex4 does not compile — needs fixes before passing

good progress — Error() fixed, tests added with strong patterns. errors.go still does not compile (return inside loop + missing end return). interfaces.go sort direction fixed but spurious copy(). One failing test (negative rectangle). Push targeted fixes for ex3+ex4 before passing.
